title: [ESI] Lower PipelineStage ops to external module instantiations
body:
This new pass lowers to instances of an external, hand-coded SV implementation. Said module is parameterized by type width and is wire-level. The RTL module will be a separate PR to reduce review load.

I tried a bunch of different approaches to this lowering, but ultimately landed here for the following reasons:
- Originally tried to (automatically) build a different RTL for each type, however the RTL dialect is not ready for sequential logic. The SV dialect would have just been a more difficult to code and understand version of the hand-written SystemVerilog.
- The "primitive" module is untyped and parameterized by width since older synthesis tools don't support parameterization by type.
- It doesn't use interfaces for the same reason.

One positive thing about using a hand-coded primitive is that it can be optimized on a per-device basis with relative ease.

Another note: the only test for this pass simply runs it and ensures that 'esi.stages' don't exist. Since this pass is not complete (this is phase 1, again to reduce reviewer load), the test will be completed in the last PR for this pass.
